texto: I stepped to the tank's edge, leaned in, and concentrated on keeping my eyes open. Which fish would be the shooter? The fish were all facing me, but one in particular seemed to be staring directly at my left eye, like a hunter targeting his prey. Wham! The water hit my pupil with such force that I jerked back …. Laughing, I wiped my left eye but stayed by the tank, my left hand resting on its edge. Another fish quickly seized the opportunity to blast the diamond in my engagement ring, while a third targeted the red carnelian stone in my earring, and yet another shot my right eye.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Virginia Morell: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a humor. O trecho “I stepped to the tank's edge, leaned in, and concentrated on keeping my eyes open. Which fish wou…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que humor, animals, archerfish pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de archerfish e biology.
